headd: Objective Test Questions: Answer all the questions

S1.headd: In each of the following sentences, there is one underlined word and one gap. From the list of words lettered A to D, choose the one that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined word and that will, at the same time, correctly fill the gap in the sentence.

S1.qs: 1.	It was a very **harmonious** meeting, without one ..................... note.
2.	The man was accused of **premeditated** murder but he pleaded that it was ......................
3.	We nearly lost the trend of his argument because of its **complexity** but the .................. of his language was our saving grace
4.	Uche achieved **ultimate** success after overcoming many ................... difficulties.
5.	I should know what to do in **normal** circumstances but this is a very .................. situation
6.	Bola is quite **extravagant** whereas his wife is .....................
7.	This is quite surprising, you cannot differentiate between the **authentic** and the ................ receipt
8.	Many people objected to the **demolition** of the toll gates whose ............. cost millions of dollars
9.	To some people, that custom is **repugnant** but to others it is ................
10.	While the armed robber remained **unruffled** after he had been arrested, his wife became ..............

S2.headd: From the words lettered A to D, choose the word that best completes each of the following sentences

S2.qs: 11.	Many people were seriously injured in the ............... accident, fortunately, nobody died.
12.	The editor decided not to publish the .................. article since the writer did not indicate his name
13.	Dende did not fulfil the requirement for admission and so was .................... from taking the entrance examination.
14.	The murder suspect denied the grave allegations against him but the evidence so far has ............... him.
15.	It was quite ................ to have invested in that new business
16.	The President granted the condemned prisoner a ..................
17.	It was difficult for the doctor to ............... the patient’s ailment.
18.	They wished her	............... of the season.
19.	Chief Koko never allows his name to be published in connection with his philanthropic activities because he ................ publicity.
20.	A befitting .................. was paid to the memory of the departed renowned professor, playwright and dramatist.

S3.headd: After each of the following sentences, a list of possible interpretations is given. Choose the interpretation that you consider most appropriate for each sentence.

S3.qs: 21.	The accused was caught red-handed by the police. This means that the accused was

opts.q0: A. made to wear red gloves
opts.q1: B. thought to be in danger
opts.q2: C. found in the very act

S3.qs: 22.	The greatest problem of the leader is that he can see no further than his nose. This means that the leader
23.	He left the country to avoid loss of face. This means that he left the country to

opts.q0: A. make his fortune somewhere else

S3.qs: 24.	John was not really angry with Janet but she seems to have taken the matter very much to heart. This means that Janet

opts.q1: B. is very upset about the matter
opts.q2: C. has taken the matter very lightly
opts.q3: D. has had a serious talk with him

S3.qs: 25.	His driving made my hair stand on end. This means that his driving was
26.	When the riot broke out in the school, only the Principal kept his head. This means that only the Principal
27.	Grace failed Mathematics last year but she has promised to turn over a new leaf this year. This means that this year Grace will

opts.q3: D. examine both sides of a leaf

S3.qs: 28.	The woman supported her husband through thick and thin. This means that she supported her husband

opts.q0: A. in both good and bad health
opts.q1: B. when he was fat and when he got thin
opts.q2: C. whether he was behaving well or badly
opts.q3: D. in both good and bad circumstances

S3.qs: 29.	Jumai thinks she is always right but I am determined to catch her out. This means that I am determined to

opts.q2: C. prove that she can make a mistake
opts.q3: D. make her agree that I am right

S3.qs: 30.	The preacher advised those who lost their property in the fire disaster not to lose hope as every cloud has a silver lining. This means that

opts.q0: A. there is a good side to all misfortunes
opts.q1: B. when there is life there is hope
opts.q2: C. to lose is a common occurrence
opts.q3: D. everybody must encounter misfortune in life

S4.headd: From the words or group of words letterred. A to D below each of the following sentences, choose the word or group of words that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word or group of words as it is used in the sentences.

S4.qs: 31.	I **concede** that I am wrong on this point
32.	Within a short time, what was meant to be a peaceful demonstration **escalated** into a riot.
33.	The students were **incensed** to hear that their school fees had been substantially increased.
34.	The flood that swept away the whole village was a **catastrophic** event
35.	The box contains the most **valuable** of all his possessions.
36.	The decision I have taken is **irrevocable**
37.	I am delighted to speak before this **august** assembly
38.	The chairman ended the meeting of the Cooperative society with **a fitting** speech
40.	The report given by the boy is **substantially** correct and we have to decide what to do about it.

S5.headd: From the word or group of words lettered A to D, choose the word or group of words that best completes each of the following sentences.

S5.qs: 41.	The new economic policy took .............. last year
42.	I'll look out ............... you at the stadium.
43.	The men were reported ............... the money fraudulently.
44.	The boy’s father made him ............... the medicine against his will
45.	The bridge was blown ................ by enemy soldiers.
46.	Chukwuma explained that he ............. home early yesterday to be able to catch the early bus.
47.	............... he comes, tell him I have left
48.	Many a player ................ to score a goal so as to be highly honoured
49.	You ...................... to know that you cannot succeed without hard work
50.	By the time I left the police station, the suspect ..................
51.	Mary has two dresses; one is dirty, ............. , is wet
52.	That house across the road is ..................., isn’t it?
53.	Mr Bello, with his wife and children, ................. to the United States
54.	The students refused to appear before the ................... panel set up to investigate the cause of the riot.
55.	The hardened criminal was convicted .................. the charges
56.	I enjoy ................ my grandson to school everyday
57.	It seems that the children are not paying attention, .....................?
58.	We knew you were not averse to ............... new friends
59.	We should have a special programme for ................... in this country
60.	Neither those girls nor that boy .................... to blame for the error
61.	The pepper has been ..................... by my sister
62.	Mr. Jato is a regular visitor of ....................
63.	I got into the room and discovered that there wasn’t ....................... there
64.	It is too abstract ................. concept for them to understand.
65.	Emmanuel is a man ................ great courage
66.	The lady enjoyed the dance .................. the fact that she had a fever.
67.	The children are all very enthusiastic ................... the forthcoming festival.
68.	Hardly had the family settled down for Mummy's special dinner ...................... the lights went off.
69.	The African extended family system gives security to .................... members
70.	The philanthropist has donated a .................... machines to the village school.
71.	Perhaps what Akin told us .............. true after all
72.	The nurse inoculated .................. pupil in the class.
73.	Well, I’ve only got ................. naira notes, but you can have twenty naira.
74.	Sule has been trying hard to live ...................... to his parent's expectations

S6.headd: In the following passages, the numbered gaps indicate missing words. Against each number in the list below each passage, four options are given in columns lettered A to D. Choose the word that is the most suitable to fill the numbered gaps in the passages.
PA: The main --75-- of malaria are regular --76-- of high --77--. The --78-- is caused by --79--, which are carried by the --80-- anopheles mosquito.

The mosquito is infected when it bites a man who is already carrying malaria parasites in his --81--; if the mosquito bites another person after about two weeks, the disease is immediately --82-- to him.
After about ten days, the man experiences general weakness, --83--, fever and sweating.
In order to prevent malaria, mosquitoes must be --84--, and their --85-- grounds must be completely destroyed.
Patients must be treated promptly, and people who travel to regions of malaria should take prophylactic --86-- to prevent --87--.

S6.headd: In the following passages the numbered gaps indicate missing words, against each number in the list below each passage four choices are given in columns lettered A to D. Choose the word that is the most suitable to fill the numbered gaps in the passage.
PB: Fishing is one of the overlooked sports in this part of the world. It really requires very little and inexpensive --88--.
While you enjoy the sport, you will be getting food for your efforts as --89-- is highly nutritious. A fisherman can use a simple pole and --90-- which are not expensive or a rod and reel, to fish.

The standared fishing method is to put a --91-- or --92-- on a hook. The fish will see this and nibble at it, strike it or snap at it. When the fish tries to eat it, the --93-- catches it in the throat.
It is also good to use --94-- for fishing equipment. These indicate when a fish is nibbling at the bait. This method is more reliable than to watch for --95-- on the water surface.
Fishermen who fish on a small --96-- do so from the river --97-- and some row in boats to the middle of the river.
Large-scale fishing needs the use of fishing --98-- which go to sea and --99-- are used to --100-- big fishes. A lot of hazards are faced by fishermen on sea.
